serviceNotes.engine oil: Mk8 continues VW’s EU fixed-interval option: inspection including oil and filter at 15,000 km or 12 months (whichever first) when the vehicle is set to time-and-distance servicing—see the digital service schedule and maintenance booklet for your market.
serviceNotes.cabin filter: Pollen filter replacement follows VW’s periodic maintenance plan, commonly at the major inspection interval (around 30,000 km / 24 months on fixed EU schedules).
serviceNotes.air filter: Air filter renewal appears on the extended inspection items in VW MQB evo maintenance tables (typically 60,000 km or 48 months for petrol models—confirm per PR number).
serviceNotes.spark plugs: TSI engines use manufacturer-specified spark plug intervals in the service schedule; 60,000 km matches common EU petrol tables—verify for eTSI/mHEV variants.
serviceNotes.DSG gearbox fluid: As on Mk7, many owners replace DSG fluid and filter around 60,000 km despite lifetime labels, to protect wet clutches and the mechatronics unit.
serviceNotes.coolant flush: Long-life coolant still ages; exchanging fluid around 120,000 km maintains corrosion protection for the electric auxiliary systems on mild-hybrid variants as well.
serviceNotes.intake carbon clean: Direct injection TSI engines benefit from periodic intake valve cleaning; 120,000 km is a typical specialist interval.
serviceNotes.brake fluid: VW specifies brake fluid replacement every 2 years. On the fixed-interval EU schedule this aligns with approximately 60,000 km.
serviceNotes.check brake pads: Brake pad inspection is part of the VW periodic maintenance programme at each major service.
